examples.sentence: The new marketing strategy needs to tie in with our existing brand message to maintain consistency.
examples.translation: The new marketing strategy needs to be related to our existing brand message to maintain consistency.

examples.sentence: Can you check if the data from the survey ties in with the results from last year’s study?
examples.translation: Can you check if the data from the survey is connected to the results from last year’s study?

examples.sentence: Her explanation didn’t really tie in with the facts we had gathered earlier.
examples.translation: Her explanation wasn’t really related to the facts we had gathered earlier.

examples.sentence: We should ensure that the budget proposal ties in with the overall financial plan for the quarter.
examples.translation: We should ensure that the budget proposal is connected to the overall financial plan for the quarter.

examples.sentence: After the conference, the team decided to tie one on and celebrate their success.
examples.translation: After the conference, the team decided to drink alcohol excessively and celebrate their success.

examples.sentence: You shouldn’t tie one on before the big presentation tomorrow; it might affect your performance.
examples.translation: You shouldn’t drink excessively before the big presentation tomorrow; it might affect your performance.

examples.sentence: He tied one on so badly last weekend that he couldn’t remember anything the next day.
examples.translation: He drank alcohol to excess last weekend and couldn’t remember anything the next day.

examples.sentence: We tied one on after the game to celebrate our championship win.
examples.translation: We celebrated by drinking heavily after the game to celebrate our championship win.

examples.sentence: Whenever there’s a holiday, the office usually ties one on to mark the occasion.
examples.translation: Whenever there’s a holiday, the office usually celebrates by drinking heavily.

examples.sentence: Let’s tie one on tonight to commemorate your promotion at work.
examples.translation: Let’s celebrate by drinking heavily tonight to commemorate your promotion at work.

examples.sentence: She carefully tied one on the back of the chair to keep the bag from falling.
examples.translation: She carefully fastened one on the back of the chair to keep the bag from falling.

examples.sentence: The sailor tied one on the mast to secure the flag during the storm.
examples.translation: The sailor fastened one on the mast to secure the flag during the storm.

examples.sentence: They had to tie one on the fence post to keep the gate from swinging open.
examples.translation: They had to fasten one on the fence post to keep the gate from swinging open.

examples.sentence: Tomorrow, I will tie one on the package before shipping it out.
examples.translation: Tomorrow, I will fasten one on the package before shipping it out.

examples.sentence: She tied herself in knots trying to explain why she missed the deadline.
examples.translation: She became extremely anxious and confused trying to explain why she missed the deadline.

examples.sentence: Don’t tie yourself in knots over the presentation; just be confident and speak clearly.
examples.translation: Don’t become extremely anxious about the presentation; just be confident and speak clearly.

examples.sentence: He tied himself in knots when the interviewer asked unexpected questions.
examples.translation: He became very anxious and agitated when the interviewer asked unexpected questions.

examples.sentence: The politician tied himself in knots during the debate, contradicting his earlier statements.
examples.translation: The politician became mentally confused and made contradictory statements during the debate.

examples.sentence: I tied myself in knots trying to justify the budget cuts to the board.
examples.translation: I became mentally entangled and confused trying to explain the budget cuts to the board.

examples.sentence: She tied herself in knots when asked about the missing documents, making things worse.
examples.translation: She became confused and produced contradictory statements when asked about the missing documents.

examples.sentence: He tied himself in knots before the big exam, worrying about every possible question.
examples.translation: He became extremely anxious and emotionally agitated before the big exam.

examples.sentence: Whenever there’s a crisis at work, she ties herself in knots trying to solve it all.
examples.translation: Whenever there’s a crisis at work, she becomes extremely anxious and emotionally agitated.

examples.sentence: They were tied in knots about the safety of their children during the storm.
examples.translation: They were extremely anxious and emotionally agitated about the safety of their children during the storm.

examples.sentence: Don’t tie yourself in knots over things beyond your control; just focus on what you can do.
examples.translation: Don’t become extremely anxious over things beyond your control; just focus on what you can do.

examples.sentence: The new regulations tie the manager’s hands, preventing her from making quick decisions.
examples.translation: The new regulations restrict the manager’s ability to make quick decisions.

examples.sentence: We feel like our hands are tied by the company’s strict policies on remote work.
examples.translation: We feel restricted by the company’s strict policies on remote work.

examples.sentence: If the budget cuts continue, it will tie our hands when it comes to hiring new staff.
examples.translation: If the budget cuts continue, it will limit our ability to hire new staff.

examples.sentence: The kidnappers tied the victim’s hands tightly before leaving the room.
examples.translation: The kidnappers restrained the victim physically by binding their hands tightly.

examples.sentence: She was tied by the wrists and left alone in the storage closet.
examples.translation: She was restrained physically by binding her wrists and left alone in the storage closet.

examples.sentence: They tied the prisoner’s hands behind his back as a precaution during transport.
examples.translation: They restrained the prisoner physically by binding his hands behind his back during transport.

examples.sentence: Before the rescue team arrived, the hostages had their hands tied to prevent escape.
examples.translation: Before the rescue team arrived, the hostages were physically restrained by having their hands tied.

examples.sentence: After dating for five years, they finally decided to tie the knot last summer.
examples.translation: After dating for five years, they finally decided to get married last summer.

examples.sentence: Are you planning to tie the knot soon, or is the wedding still a way off?
examples.translation: Are you planning to get married soon, or is the wedding still a way off?

examples.sentence: They will be tying the knot in a small ceremony next month with close family only.
examples.translation: They will be getting married in a small ceremony next month with close family only.

examples.sentence: Make sure you tie the knot tightly on that rope before climbing the tree.
examples.translation: Make sure you fasten the knot tightly on that rope before climbing the tree.

examples.sentence: He tied the knot of his shoelaces before heading out for the run.
examples.translation: He fastened the knot of his shoelaces before heading out for the run.

examples.sentence: They had to tie several knots to secure the tarp over the equipment during the storm.
examples.translation: They had to fasten several knots to secure the tarp over the equipment during the storm.

examples.sentence: Tomorrow, I will teach you how to tie the knot for fishing lines properly.
examples.translation: Tomorrow, I will teach you how to fasten the knot for fishing lines properly.

examples.sentence: She tied up the parcels with string before sending them to the post office.
examples.translation: She fastened the parcels with string before sending them to the post office.

examples.sentence: The workers tied up the boxes securely to prevent damage during transport.
examples.translation: The workers fastened the boxes securely to prevent damage during transport.

examples.sentence: I’m going to tie up the cords behind the TV to keep them organized.
examples.translation: I’m going to fasten the cords behind the TV to keep them organized.

examples.sentence: Before leaving, he tied up the tent ropes tightly to withstand the wind.
examples.translation: Before leaving, he fastened the tent ropes tightly to withstand the wind.

examples.sentence: The captain tied up the yacht at the marina after a long day at sea.
examples.translation: The captain secured the yacht to the dock at the marina after a long day at sea.

examples.sentence: We tied up the boat to the pier before going ashore for dinner.
examples.translation: We moored the boat to the pier before going ashore for dinner.

examples.sentence: They will tie up the fishing vessel alongside the larger ship for unloading.
examples.translation: They will secure the fishing vessel alongside the larger ship for unloading.

examples.sentence: Make sure to tie up the dinghy so it doesn’t drift away with the tide.
examples.translation: Make sure to secure the dinghy so it doesn’t drift away with the tide.

examples.sentence: I can’t take on any more projects right now; my schedule is tied up completely.
examples.translation: I can’t take on any more projects right now; my schedule is fully occupied.

examples.sentence: The funds are tied up in long-term investments and aren’t available for new expenses.
examples.translation: The funds are committed in long-term investments and aren’t available for new expenses.

examples.sentence: We were tied up with last-minute preparations and couldn’t meet you for lunch.
examples.translation: We were occupied with last-minute preparations and couldn’t meet you for lunch.
